mnemonics: Solve First Order Linear: Find Home (Homogeneous) first, then a Particular (Particular) solution to move into.
keyPoints: A first order linear recurrence has the form: aₙ = c * aₙ₋₁ + f(n)
The solution is the sum of the homogeneous solution (aₙ⁽ʰ⁾) and a particular solution (aₙ⁽ᵖ⁾)
The homogeneous solution, for constant c, is aₙ⁽ʰ⁾ = A * cⁿ, where A is a constant determined by the initial condition.
The form of the particular solution is guessed based on the form of f(n) (the non-homogeneous part).
commonMistakes: Forgetting to add the homogeneous and particular solutions together for the final general solution.
Using the wrong guess for the particular solution (e.g., using a constant guess when f(n) is an exponential that matches the homogeneous solution).
Applying the method to a relation that is not linear (e.g., aₙ = aₙ₋₁ * aₙ₋₂) or not first order.
